channel.title: Unidentified Flying Objects on Alien Truth Social
channel.description: Recent content in Unidentified Flying Objects on Alien Truth Social

item.title: Pentagon Releases Batch of Unidentified Anomalous Phenomena Files
item.description:
The Pentagon has released approximately 160 declassified documents concerning unidentified anomalous phenomena (UAP), including never-before-seen footage and historical materials dating back to the 1940s. These files contain reports of various UAP sightings, such as “orbs launching orbs” and a “misshapen and uneven ball of white light,” as well as accounts from Apollo crews. The release aims to provide public transparency, though the government has not claimed to have detected extraterrestrial life. Public opinion suggests a belief that the government may be concealing information about UAP, with some thinking they are alien in origin.

Source: https://www.wired.com/story/pentagon-drops-new-ufo-files

item.title: Pentagon Releases Files Related to Unidentified Anomalous Phenomena (UAP)
item.description:
The Pentagon has begun releasing government files concerning unidentified anomalous phenomena (UAP), also known as UFOs. These files include FBI reports on unexplained sightings and NASA debriefs with astronauts who observed unidentified objects on the moon. Images released alongside the files depict various phenomena, including an object seen by a U.S. military operator over the Middle East in May 2022 and a football-shaped UAP reported near Japan in 2024. Archival photographs from the Apollo 12 and Apollo 17 missions also show unidentified phenomena on the lunar surface.

Source: https://www.scientificamerican.com/article/see-the-pentagons-new-ufo-image-release

item.title: UFO (1956 film)
item.description:
UFO, also known as Unidentified Flying Objects: The True Story of Flying Saucers, is a 1956 semi-documentary that explores the history of the UFO phenomenon in the United States. Through a blend of reenactments and analysis, the film follows the perspective of Air Force press officer Albert M. Chop as he transitions from a skeptic to a believer in the reality of unidentified aircraft. The production is notable for featuring real-life figures and actual footage, such as the famous Mariana UFO incident, and has served as a primary source for subsequent media coverage of the topic.

Source: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/UFO_%281956_film%29

item.title: Reports from Project Grudge and Project Blue Book
item.description:
Project Grudge was a U.S. Air Force project that investigated UFOs from February to December 1949. Its stated goal was to alleviate public anxiety by explaining UFO sightings as conventional phenomena, mass hysteria, hoaxes, or psychopathological individuals. The project’s sole formal report concluded there was no evidence of foreign development and recommended reducing the scope of UFO investigations. Critics like J. Allen Hynek and Edward J. Ruppelt argued Project Grudge was more of a public relations campaign than an objective scientific examination.

Source: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Project_Grudge

item.title: Project Sign report
item.description:
Project Sign, also known as Project Saucer, was a U.S. Air Force study of unidentified flying objects active for most of 1948. Established to collect and evaluate UFO sighting information due to potential national security concerns, it was the precursor to Project Grudge. A public paper released in 1949 stated that while some UFOs appeared to be actual aircraft, their origins could not be determined due to insufficient data. The project investigated claims related to inventor Jonathan Edward Caldwell’s disk-rotor aircraft, but found they could not explain the widespread “flying saucer” reports.

Source: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Project_Sign

item.title: World UFO Day
item.description:
World UFO Day is an awareness day observed annually by some on June 24 and others on July 2. The June date commemorates the first widely reported UFO sighting in the United States, while the July date marks the Roswell incident. The July 2 observance, declared by the World UFO Day Organisation, aims to raise awareness of UFOs and encourage governments to declassify related files. Celebrations involve sky-watching and discussions about the possibility of extraterrestrial life.

Source: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/World_UFO_Day

item.title: COMETA Report
item.description:
The COMETA report is an unofficial 1999 publication by the French association COMETA concerning unidentified aerospace phenomena (UAP). Titled “UFOs and Defense: What Should We Prepare For?”, the report compiled UAP/UFO incidents over approximately 60 years, focusing on national defense implications. Contributors included defense experts and former officials from the French Air Force and space agency. The report concluded that a significant percentage of well-documented cases could not be explained by known sources, suggesting the extraterrestrial hypothesis as the most probable explanation for certain phenomena.

Source: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/COMETA_report

item.title: Flying Saucers from Outer Space
item.description:
Flying Saucers from Outer Space is a non-fiction book about UFOs written by Donald Keyhoe and published on October 5, 1953. The book was followed by The Flying Saucer Conspiracy in 1955 and preceded by The Flying Saucers Are Real in 1950. In 1956, a science-fiction film titled Earth vs. the Flying Saucers was made, suggested by Keyhoe’s book.

Source: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Flying_Saucers_from_Outer_Space
